records.ety: [AS. defol, deful; akin to G. teufel, Goth. diabalus; all fr. L. diabolus the devil, Gr. dia`bolos the devil, the slanderer, fr. diaba`llw to slander, calumniate, orig., to throw across; dia` across + ba`llw to throw, let fall, fall; cf. Skr. gal to fall. Cf. Diabolic.]

defs: The Evil One; Satan, represented as the tempter and spiritual of mankind.
quotes: [Jesus] being forty days tempted of the devil.
That old serpent, called the Devil, and Satan, which deceiveth the whole world.
A dumb man possessed with a devil.

defs: A very wicked person; hence, any great evil.
quotes: Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?

defs: An expletive of surprise, vexation, or emphasis, or, ironically, of negation.
quotes: The devil a puritan that he is, . . . but a timepleaser.
The things, we know, are neither rich nor rare, But wonder how the devil they got there.

defs: A dish, as a bone with the meat, broiled and excessively peppered; a grill with Cayenne pepper.
quotes: Men and women busy in baking, broiling, roasting oysters, and preparing devils on the gridiron.

defs: A machine for tearing or cutting rags, cotton, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deviled (?) or Devilled; p. pr. & vb. n. Deviling (?) or Devilling.]

defs: To make like a devil; to invest with the character of a devil.
To grill with Cayenne pepper; to season highly in cooking, as with pepper.
